skill.name: baseline-extraction-for-clinical-trials
skill.description: Extracts clinical trial baseline data (study, region, participants, etc.) from article text or PMID. Checks PubMed for metadata; always falls back to LLM extraction for full details.
skill.summary: A hybrid workflow that checks PubMed for clinical trial metadata and uses LLM analysis to extract 10 structured baseline characteristics from article text.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 58 static analysis findings were evaluated and determined to be FALSE POSITIVES. The 16 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings (references/extraction_schema.md, SKILL.md, scripts/extract_pdf.py) are field descriptions and markdown formatting, not crypto algorithms. The 6 'System reconnaissance' findings are standard skill descriptions and a legacy audit JSON file. The 29 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings flag markdown code block formatting in SKILL.md, not shell commands. The 'Python file write/append' finding (scripts/extract_pdf.py:53) is legitimate PDF text extraction. The 'subprocess.run' example (SKILL.md:71) is example code in documentation. The 'Hardcoded URL' (SKILL.md:7) is the source repository attribution. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or obfuscated code was found. This skill passed a previous legacy audit with a 'Production Ready' grade (87/100). The technical risk patterns (filesystem write, network references) are present but represent legitimate documented functionality.

content.user_title: Extract Clinical Trial Baseline Data from Articles
content.value_statement: Researchers need a repeatable method to extract structured baseline data from clinical trial publications. This skill provides a hybrid workflow that checks PubMed for metadata and uses LLM analysis to capture all 10 required baseline characteristics.
actual_capabilities: Extract 10 key baseline characteristics from clinical trial article text
Look up PubMed metadata using a provided PMID for verification
Fall back to LLM-based extraction when PubMed data is incomplete or unavailable
Process PDF files to extract text content using the included helper script
Generate structured JSON output following a predefined extraction schema
Distinguish between found and missing fields to prevent data hallucination
limitations: Cannot access PubMed for PMIDs without internet connectivity
PDF text extraction quality depends on the source PDF format and encoding
Does not perform statistical analysis or meta-analysis on extracted data
LLM extraction accuracy varies for poorly described or ambiguous trial details

use_cases.title: Systematic Review Data Collection
use_cases.description: Medical researchers conducting systematic reviews can extract standardized baseline characteristics from multiple clinical trial articles, ensuring consistent data across studies.

use_cases.title: Meta-Analysis Preparation
use_cases.description: Research teams preparing meta-analyses can collect structured baseline data from published trials to build the input dataset for statistical synthesis.

use_cases.title: Clinical Trial Registry Verification
use_cases.description: Clinical data managers can verify that published trial results match registry entries by comparing extracted baseline data against PubMed records.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Baseline Extraction from Article Text
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the 10 baseline characteristics from this clinical trial article text. Follow the extraction schema from references/extraction_schema.md. Return the result as a JSON object. Set any missing fields to null.
prompt_templates.scenario: User provides article text without a PMID

prompt_templates.title: PMID Lookup with LLM Fallback
prompt_templates.prompt: Look up PMID 12345678 using the PubMed check workflow. If the result is incomplete or not found, fall back to LLM extraction using the full article text provided below. Return the best available result in JSON format.
prompt_templates.scenario: User provides a PMID and article text

prompt_templates.title: PDF Text Extraction Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Run scripts/extract_pdf.py on the provided PDF file to extract text. Then analyze the extracted text using the baseline extraction schema. Return the structured JSON output.
prompt_templates.scenario: User provides a PDF file path

prompt_templates.title: Custom Field Extraction with Modified Schema
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract baseline data from this clinical trial article. Include all 10 standard fields from the schema and add an extra field for funding source if mentioned. Mark any unavailable fields as null.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs additional fields beyond the standard schema

output_examples.input: PMID lookup for trial 34567890 with full article text provided
output_examples.output: Structured JSON output containing study author and year, region, participant count, sex distribution, average age, population description, intervention details, comparator information, outcome measures, and study design classification

output_examples.input: Full text of a randomized controlled trial for diabetes treatment
output_examples.output: JSON object with all 10 baseline fields populated from the article text, with any unreported fields set to null and documented assumptions noted in the summary

output_examples.input: PDF file of a clinical trial publication named trial_results.pdf
output_examples.output: Plain text file (trial_results_fulltext.txt) extracted from the PDF, followed by structured baseline data in JSON format with study design labeled as RCT

best_practices: Always provide the PMID when available for more reliable PubMed metadata lookup
Review the extracted output against the original source to catch extraction errors
Document any fields that were set to null due to missing information in the source
anti_patterns: Relying only on LLM extraction without checking PubMed first when a PMID is available
Using the extraction result as published data without manual verification
Modifying the output schema fields without updating the extraction schema reference

faq.question: What baseline fields does this skill extract?
faq.answer: It extracts 10 fields: study, region, number of participants, sex, age, population, intervention or exposure, comparator or context, outcome, and study design.

faq.question: What happens if the PMID is not found in PubMed?
faq.answer: The skill falls back to LLM extraction from the full article text to capture the required baseline fields.

faq.question: Can this skill process PDF files directly?
faq.answer: Yes, use the extract_pdf.py script to convert PDF files to text before running the extraction workflow.

faq.question: What should I do if I do not have a PMID?
faq.answer: Provide the full article text directly. The skill will skip the PubMed step and use LLM extraction for all fields.

faq.question: How does the skill handle missing or unclear data?
faq.answer: Fields that are not mentioned in the source text are set to null or empty string instead of hallucinated values.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for non-randomized trials?
faq.answer: Yes, the schema includes a study_design field that can accommodate RCT, cohort, case-control, and other study types.
